message:
feat(fuzz): add libfuzzer harnesses for session entry, SSE parser, and SSE stream

Adds three new coverage-guided fuzz targets under fuzz/fuzz_targets/:

1. fuzz_session_entry.rs — SessionEntry JSON deserialization fuzzing
   Feeds arbitrary bytes through serde_json::from_str::<SessionEntry>()
   to find panics in the deserialization path. For successfully parsed
   entries, performs a serialize→deserialize round-trip and asserts
   discriminant stability (the entry variant survives the round-trip).
   Also tests BOM-prefixed input and line-oriented parsing (up to 64
   lines) to simulate JSONL corruption patterns where individual lines
   may be malformed.

2. fuzz_sse_parser.rs — SseParser chunking invariant fuzzing
   Tests the critical chunking invariant: parsing a complete SSE stream
   in one feed() call MUST produce identical events as feeding the same
   input character-by-character or split at an arbitrary midpoint. This
   catches state machine bugs where internal buffering produces
   different results depending on chunk boundaries. Three strategies:
   - Whole input in one call
   - Character-by-character feeding
   - Split at midpoint (two chunks)
   All three must produce identical event sequences and flush results.

3. fuzz_sse_stream.rs — Raw byte-level SSE processing fuzzing
   Unlike fuzz_sse_parser (which operates on valid UTF-8), this harness
   feeds raw arbitrary bytes including intentionally malformed UTF-8
   sequences, testing the recovery paths that SseStream uses when
   receiving wire data. Simulates the UTF-8 accumulate-decode-buffer
   pattern from SseStream::process_chunk() with varying chunk sizes
   (1 to 64 bytes, cycling). Separately tests lossy UTF-8 conversion
   as a baseline. Must never panic on any input.

All three targets use libfuzzer_sys and import from pi::fuzz_exports.

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>
